Draft | Data-backed fact base

Barclays vs Peers — neutral baseline to support leadership brainstorming

Lite version: All charts/images removed except one, to support sharing in locked-down environments.

Scope: peer-set comparisons using FY-12/2023–FY-12/2025 reported metrics and valuation multiples as of 02/2026 (per provided dataset).

How to use this pack

  • Start with the facts: where Barclays is strong vs peers, where gaps remain.
  • Then discuss drivers: what appears to be driving the deltas (growth, efficiency, credit, mix).
  • Then brainstorm: interventions that could move the metrics (X → Y → Z).

What’s included

  • 12 narrative slides (non-interactive HTML)
  • Appendix tables: underlying peer data for quick reference during discussion
Discussion goal: align on the few metrics that matter most and the questions we need to answer to improve them.
Source: peer benchmark dataset (uploaded text extracts). Prepared for consulting leadership discussion.
Executive fact pattern

Momentum is strong, but the dataset still shows a valuation discount vs peers

Analyst sentiment
75% Buy / 25% Hold
Analyst Recommendation
Share price growth
19.8%
Peer pack
1-year TRS growth
48.4%
TRS Growth
P/BV (02/2026)
0.9699261287548491x
Market Cap to BV
P/E (02/2026)
8.084715598685742x
Market Cap to BV
Prompt: What outcomes would need to improve for Barclays to sustain momentum and narrow the discount?
Key Barclays FY-12/2025 reference points: operating income growth 10.9%; pre-tax ROE 12.5%; cost/income 59.9%; loan loss ratio 7.8%; CET1 14.3%.
Peer baseline

Within this peer set, higher valuation aligns with stronger sustained profitability

What this suggests (from the data)

  • Peers with higher pre-tax ROE profiles tend to carry higher P/BV multiples in the dataset.
  • Barclays sits in a mid-range ROE position with a sub-1.0x P/BV multiple.
Prompt: Which profitability drivers matter most for Barclays’ position (efficiency, credit, mix, pricing)?
ROE: FY-12/2025. P/BV: 02/2026. Source: Pre-tax ROE Decomposition; Market Cap to BV Decomposition.
Growth

Operating income growth is a relative strength for Barclays in FY-12/2025

Chart removed for lightweight shareable version.

Callouts

  • Barclays FY-12/2025 operating income growth: 10.9%.
  • Multi-year context: 3-year operating income CAGR is reported in the appendix.
Prompt: What is driving the growth (mix, volumes, pricing) — and which parts are most resilient?
Source: Operating Inc_ Growth (Operating Income section).
Growth drivers

Growth appears broad-based across net interest and non-interest income lines

Chart removed for lightweight shareable version.
Chart removed for lightweight shareable version.
Prompt: Which income drivers should we pressure-test (rate sensitivity, fee sustainability, trading cyclicality, customer mix)?
Source: Operating Inc_ Growth (Net Interest Income & Non-interest Income sections). Peer median computed from provided peer set.
Conversion to returns

Profitability is improving, but returns remain mid-range vs best-in-class peers

Chart removed for lightweight shareable version.

Interpretation for discussion

  • Barclays’ FY-12/2025 pre-tax ROE is 12.5%.
  • ROA and EBT margin provide additional “conversion” signals (appendix).
Prompt: Where is value “leaking” between growth and returns (cost, credit, balance sheet efficiency, mix)?
Source: Pre-tax ROE Decomposition; Return on Assets. Peer medians computed from provided peer set.
Efficiency

Cost / income ratio improvement is visible in the dataset

Chart removed for lightweight shareable version.

Why it matters (data link)

  • Cost/income is directly linked to EBT margin in the dataset tables.
  • Barclays FY-12/2025 cost/income: 59.9%.
Prompt: Which cost-to-serve drivers are most material (staff vs other costs), and what questions should we ask to explain the FY-12/2025 improvement?
Source: Pre-tax ROE Decomposition (Cost/Income, Staff Cost/Income, Occupancy & other cost ratios in appendix).
Risk / credit

Credit cost stands out as a headwind vs peers (FY-12/2025)

Chart removed for lightweight shareable version.

Barclays indicators (FY-12/2025)

  • Loan loss ratio: 7.8% (peer set comparison in chart)
  • Loan loss / net interest income: see appendix (useful for “coverage” view)
  • Segment detail: US Consumer loan loss ratio is notably high (appendix)
Prompt: What interventions reduce losses while preserving franchise value (early warning, collections, policy, data/decisioning)?
Source: Pre-tax ROE Decomposition; Business Segment Analysis (Barclays-only segment view).
Concentration

Operating income is concentrated in Investment Bank + UK; Americas is meaningful

Chart removed for lightweight shareable version.
Chart removed for lightweight shareable version.
Prompt: Which 1–2 parts of the business have the largest “delta to peers” and are most actionable?
Source: Business Segment Analysis; Geographic Segment Analysis (shares of operating income shown in provided extracts).
Balance sheet context

Deposits grew faster than loans; net interest margin remains comparatively low

Chart removed for lightweight shareable version.
Chart removed for lightweight shareable version.
Prompt: What mix, pricing, or funding questions should we ask to understand (and potentially improve) NIM/ROA without undue risk?
Source: Loan _ Deposit Growth (CAGR); Pre-tax ROE Decomposition (Net Interest Margin).
Resilience / flexibility

Capital position appears strong within the peer set

Chart removed for lightweight shareable version.
Chart removed for lightweight shareable version.
Prompt: What strategic options become more viable given capital strength — and what constraints should we validate?
Source: Regulatory Ratios (CET1 and Total Capital Ratio).
Structured brainstorming (no selling)

Discussion prompts tied to metrics in the pack (X → Y → Z)

Use this as a facilitation page. Keep ideas anchored to a metric and a measurable leading indicator.

Metric in the dataWhat might move it (X)Leading indicators (Y)Outcome (Z)
Loan loss ratio (esp. US Consumer)Underwriting/collections/early warning improvementsDelinquency, cure rates, loss severityLower provisions → higher EBT/ROE
Cost / incomeReduce unit cost-to-serve; simplify processes; automate controlsCycle time, straight-through processing, manual handoffsHigher EBT margin and operating leverage
NIM / ROAPricing discipline; balance sheet mix; funding optimizationDeposit beta, product mix, spread by segmentHigher ROA → higher ROE
Operating income growthSustain growth engines; improve conversion to profitSegment mix, margin by segment, customer retentionDurable profitability supports re-rating
Prompts are derived from the metrics shown in this pack (credit, efficiency, NIM/ROA, growth-to-returns conversion).

BNP Paribas SA 79% 16% 5%
